title: Multiple viewport flexible screen
abstract: Described herein are systems, apparatus, methods and computer program products configured to detect bends within a flexible display and utilize the flexible display to provide a plurality of viewports. A geometry identifier may be electrically coupled to the flexible display and allow for determination of bends of the flexible display and for segregating the display into a plurality of viewport based on the bends.
claims:
Claims 1. An apparatus comprising: a flexible display, configured to bend in at least one direction; a geometry identifier, electrically coupled to the flexible display and configured to generate an electrical signal associated with bending of at least a portion of the flexible display; a controller, communicatively coupled to the geometry identifier and configured to perform operations comprising: receiving the electrical signal; determining, based on the electrical signal, a first bend of the flexible display; segregating, based on the determining, the display into at least a first viewport and a second viewport; and displaying a first view within the first viewport and a second view within the second viewport. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the geometry identifier is configured to measure electrical potential differences within portions of the flexible display, and wherein the geometry identifier is configured to generate the electrical signal based on the electrical potential differences. 3. The apparatus of claim 1 or 2, wherein the geometry identifier comprises a potentiometer. 4. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-3, further comprising: a bulge detector, configured to determine bulges within at least a portion of the flexible display, wherein the operations further comprise: determining a first bulge of the flexible display; and determining that the first bulge is not indicative of a second bend of the flexible display. 5. The apparatus of claim 4, wherein the bulge detector is configured to determine that an electrical signal generated by the geometry identifier is below a threshold signal reading. 6. The apparatus of claim 4, wherein the segregating the display into at least the first viewport and the second viewport is further based on the determining that the first bulge is not indicative of a second bend of the flexible display. 7. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the first viewport and the second viewport are segregated along the first bend. 8. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the segregating the display into at least the first viewport and the second viewport comprises: determining a larger portion between the first viewport and the second viewport; and designating the first viewport as a primary viewport, wherein the first view comprises a view of an application. 9. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the determining the larger portion comprises determining that the first viewport is larger than half of a total display area of the flexible display. 10. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the segregating the display into at least the first viewport and the second viewport comprises: determining a smaller portion between the first viewport and the second viewport: and designating the second viewport as a secondary viewport, wherein the second view comprises a secondary view. 11. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-10, wherein the segregating the display into at least the first viewport and the second viewport comprises: determining that the second viewport is oriented at a threshold angle away from the first viewport; and designating the second viewport as a non-visible viewport. 12. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-11, wherein the operations further comprise: determining, based on the electrical signal, a second bend of the flexible display; further segregating, based on the determining the first bend and the determining the second bend, the display into at least a third viewport; and displaying a third view within the third viewport. 13. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the operations further comprise: determining that the third viewport is irregularly shaped; and designating the third viewport as an irregular viewport. 14. An method comprising: receiving an electrical signal from a geometry identifier electrically coupled to a flexible display, the electrical signal associated with bending of at least a portion of a flexible display; determining, based on the electrical signal, a first bend of the flexible display; segregating, based on the determining, the display into at least a first viewport and a second viewport; and displaying a first view within the first viewport and a second view within the second viewport. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the geometry identifier is configured to measure electrical potential differences within portions of the flexible 

display, wherein the geometry identifier is configured to generate the electrical signal is based on the electrical potential differences, wherein the geometry identifier comprises a potentiometer, and wherein the method further comprises: determining, with a bulge detector configured to determine bulges within at least a portion of the flexible display by determining that an electrical signal generated by the geometry identifier is below a threshold signal reading, a first bulge of the flexible display; and determining that the first bulge is not indicative of a second bend of the flexible display, wherein the segregating the display into at least the first viewport and the second viewport is further based on the determining that the first bulge is not indicative of a second bend of the flexible display.
